reason: The market is exhibiting strong signs of distribution and profit-taking, failing to sustain rallies above $70,000 despite favorable institutional news. The short-lived jump to $74,000 was quickly met with selling pressure, indicating significant resistance. Broader market fragility, evidenced by issues with a BlackRock private credit fund, is impacting crypto prices and DeFi. Additionally, ongoing regulatory scrutiny adds a layer of uncertainty and risk, suggesting potential for further downside or consolidation rather than a sustained uptrend.
headlines: Why bitcoin couldn't hold $70,000 despite its best week of Wall Street news in months
Managing financial AI agents is the only skill you’ll need to survive the AI layoffs
BlackRock private credit fund is latest to crack, hitting crypto prices and DeFi markets
Bitcoin buyers are cashing out fast after short-lived jump to $74,000
Binance tells Senate probe no accounts sent crypto directly to Iran
